Discover curated, free images for your projects!
Explore Lummi

Open Peeps

A hand-drawn illustration library.

Use with BlushDownload

Open Peeps by Pablo Stanley.
Free for commercial and personal use under CC0 License.

Mix & Match.

The library works like building blocks made of vector arms, legs, and emotions. You can mix these elements to create different Peeps.

  • Combine clothing and hairstyles to add flair.
  • Change emotion with different facial expressions.
  • Set the scene with different poses—including standing and sitting
Download Library

Creations for all occasions.

You can use Open Peeps in product illustration, marketing imagery, comics, product states, user flows, personas, storyboarding, invitations for your quinceañera...or anything else not on this list.

The library is in the public domain under the CC0 License. This means you can copy, modify, distribute, remix, burn, and use the work, even for commercial purposes, without asking permission.

Download Library

How to mix a Peep.

Creating a character is easy! Use any product design tool like Sketch, Figma, XD, or Studio to mix nested components.
There are over 584,688 possible combinations (yup, someone did the math!)

Top them off

You can choose curly, long, short, or no hair at all.  

Give the gift of vision

Choose from clear lenses and sunnies.

Add a feeling

Put an emotion on that beautiful face.

Select a body

Body language is just as important!

Add facial hair

Everyone loves a mustache—or beard, or...you get it.

Change the colors

The black and white colors are just a starting point—you can customize them!

Grab and go!

Get started with these ready-to-download Peeps.

Check out what others are making!

wow @pablostanley it looks exactly like me pic.twitter.com/b1EsPRs0pH

— welcome to the @jongold, we got fun & games (@jongold) February 26, 2020

@pablostanley We just added some fresh #OpenPeeps to our website -- check out https://t.co/8xxuFBvoJZ for the full effect. ❤️😉 pic.twitter.com/BnzFnN4J0v

— UX Design Contest (@UXDcontest) February 27, 2020

ooh got excited trying out Open Peeps. man, love the diversity! there are so many options! thanks @pablostanley you da best! 👍🏻👌🏻 pic.twitter.com/Kk5fhHffmU

— Jeorge Paolo (@jeorgepaolo_) February 27, 2020

👈 You can make actually pretty rad avatars with this!

— Kristy Marcinova (@iKristy) February 26, 2020

I am so excited about my first physical project board!

Used some of @pablostanley’s #openpeeps to keep it interesting! pic.twitter.com/p5RatVHGAr

— Lizzi (@LizziStanding) February 26, 2020

How I feel about @pablostanley's Open Peeps project. pic.twitter.com/RbJLk6EWlT

— Papaya Creative, LLC (@papaya_creative) February 26, 2020

Using them Open Peeps for my school project!! @pablostanley

Absolutely in love!! 😍 pic.twitter.com/dUQYcUQQEF

@jeevjyot Configured an avatar for you using Open Peeps illustrations by @pablostanley

I hope you'll like it :D#avatar #illustration #sketchtogether pic.twitter.com/9uiaGlmFe4

— Tanveer Singh Mahendra (@mtanveersingh) February 26, 2020

Made by Open Peeps @pablostanley #NewProfilePicture pic.twitter.com/gpKv1sHm5a

— NOB NUKUI (@nobtaka) February 26, 2020

Used @pablostanley's Open Peeps library and @figmadesign (absolute newbie) to create my @NotionHQ-inspired icon. THRILLED!

One day, my signature cat-eye glasses will be inserted into the image💜🧡💚 https://t.co/NQYfEeaBri pic.twitter.com/FTWW9KA8HJ

— Rui Qing (@ruiqing) February 28, 2020

pic.twitter.com/19Bw6kElD5

— yahalo (@paswotnya) February 27, 2020

pic.twitter.com/W7as8iAcZy

— Adam Ruf 💪❤️🤜🤛💼👍 (@ux_adam) February 26, 2020

Thanks for this! Here’s my avatar.

Also, this is probably the best place to ask – has anyone researched on what’s the benefit of having an illustration as a profile image.

Does it create a larger than life brand for the individual or some other crazy thing? pic.twitter.com/Vzuqsl7QHN

— Kavir (@KavirKaycee) February 26, 2020

#newprofilepic courtesy @pablostanley pic.twitter.com/N6sdMkN8mo

— imad ali (@1madal1) February 28, 2020

YES. Already used the monochromatic for our new tiny team image (tho our Mexican designer is disappointed with it because she's heavily tattooed). pic.twitter.com/gBb2Fe4YDU

— Dylan Wilbanks, Human Grumpy Cat (@dylanw) February 28, 2020

Some plans are meant not be to planned when you are developer 😂
My First Developer Stories.. @pablostanley pic.twitter.com/4cV2lGac48

— Akshat (@shrmaky) February 29, 2020

@pablostanley Worked on #AmazonGo 's liquor checkout using #openpeeps. Great tool to make refined storyboards! pic.twitter.com/fjlPpPPqXb

— Harika Bommu (@harikabommu) March 10, 2020

Start creating.

Download the illustration library and create your own Peeps!

Use with BlushDownload

Open Peeps by Pablo Stanley. Part of the Open Doodles project.
Free for commercial and personal use under CC0 License.
Oh, btw, you should check out Lummi for more free illustrations.

Made with Webflow
